profile.name: paper-reading
profile.intent: Support reading-only projects, focused source intake, source cards, corpus comparison, and literature maps without activating experiment execution or full paper-writing machinery.

routing.handoff_policy: Enter through discovery-router, then use reference-library-manager, reference-reading-summarizer, reference-project-synthesizer, reference-corpus-analyzer, or literature-review-sprint according to corpus size and output depth.

install_policy.notes: Reading-only projects should not install experiment, rebuttal, release, or full ML paper lifecycle skills by default.

workflow_contract.lane_selection_rule: Use evidence for source reading, standard or heavy mode for durable literature decisions, combined when source-library files or project memory are updated, and no-contract only for informal read-only answers.

required_preflight: Classify source visibility before writing source cards or summaries.
Check whether source files, reference cards, or project memory already exist.
Use source-card compression before promoting claims into project memory or public docs.
forbidden_actions: Do not copy long copyrighted passages into public artifacts.
Do not promote raw private notes or reading trajectories without publication audit.
Do not activate experiment or code-execution workflows for a reading-only project unless the user changes scope.
action_lane.approval_boundary: Escalate before publishing summaries, importing private notes, changing source visibility, or turning reading output into paper claims.

evidence_lane.confidence_policy: Distinguish skimmed, partial, and deep-read sources; do not make durable novelty or citation-coverage claims without enough source coverage.
evidence_lane.citation_policy: Use source cards and citation keys for durable claims; keep quotes short and avoid raw copyrighted text in public memory.

combined_lane.handoff_artifact: source card, project-use note, literature map, or corpus matrix
combined_lane.staleness_trigger: New papers, updated source files, missing citation metadata, or changed project scope.

no_contract_lane.allowed_when: The request is a transient, low-stakes explanation that does not write source cards, project memory, or citation decisions.
no_contract_lane.escalation_trigger: The answer will become a durable source card, literature decision, project claim, public summary, or publication-facing citation recommendation.

acceptance_checks: Kernel example references the paper-reading profile and discovery-router entrypoint.
Evidence lane defines source-ledger, citation, and source-visibility boundaries.
No experiment-execution or full ML lifecycle skills are required by default.

privacy_boundary.publication_audit_required_when: Use memory-publication-auditor before publishing private notes, raw reading traces, or source-derived patterns as reusable skills.

selection_contexts: Select paper-reading directly for reading-only source intake, source-card, literature map, or corpus comparison projects.
Keep experiment execution and full paper-writing machinery out of the default route unless the task explicitly asks for them.
selection_semantics.delegation_policy: After selection, delegate to discovery-router, reference-library-manager, reference-reading-summarizer, reference-project-synthesizer, reference-corpus-analyzer, or literature-review-sprint as appropriate.
selection_semantics.installable_manifest_gate: Installable manifests should preserve reading-only selection before leaf-skill routing and keep the profile independent from ML experiment execution.

promotion_gates: Reading-only projects can run source-card and corpus workflows without the full ML research pack.
Source visibility, citation policy, and reference-memory writeback remain intact outside ml-research-skills.
Profile routing evals show paper-reading can be selected before leaf-skill routing.
rejection_checks: Do not extract if reading workflows still require experiment or paper-submission machinery by default.
Do not publish private notes or raw copyrighted text in kernel examples.
Do not let runtime-specific adapters replace the Markdown/YAML/JSON source of truth.
